calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-17-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bart Flood for the (co-administrators) Brigid Saulny & Adrienne Baker.

The Court stated that this is on for extraordinary attorney's fees, that it has read the declarations, asked if there was any further argument regarding the fees, that it has concerns regarding the community property, car loan and the estate of the deceased mother. Mr. Flood addressed the community property stating that there is a family relationship issue, that there are two adult children of the deceased wife that were never adopted, if they qualify as stepchildren, that the family did not want them to take from the estate, that he talked to family members to see if the decedent ever held them out as his children and was told no, that it is a strained relationship and cited 6402.5 in that they are entitled to their mother's interest in the real property. Mr. Flood addressed the car loan explaining that the decedent did not have liquid assets, that the collection agent was aggressive and was trying to repossess, that the vehicle had equity and the administrators did not want to lose the asset, that it was refinanced under Brigid's name and incurred the payments as well as fees for insurance and storage, that they were worried about foreclosure, that the attorney had to research 9391, that the co-administrator's had never dealt with this type of situation and the attorney had to negotiate with the finance company. Mr. Flood addressed the death of Helen C. Baker stating that prior to her death there was concern as to if receiving the distribution would jeopardize her public benefits, that when she past away he had to communicate with her attorney in Minnesota and that it was first thought that the funds could go into her trust account. The Accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Lynn O'Malley Taylor. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Nancy Wong. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
